treatyParticipationCards.title: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: The first global standard-setting instrument on AI ethics. Sets universal principles on human rights, dignity, fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, oversight, and sustainability. Adopted by UNESCO's General Conference on 23 November 2021; broadly cited by national regulators and regional bodies.

treatyParticipationCards.title: UN General Assembly Resolution 78/265 "Safe, secure and trustworthy AI systems for sustainable development"
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: political_guidance
treatyParticipationCards.summary: First broad UNGA resolution focused on safe, secure and trustworthy AI, reaffirming international law and framing AI through sustainable development and human rights. Adopted by consensus.

treatyParticipationCards.title: UN General Assembly Resolution 78/311 "Enhancing international cooperation on capacity-building of AI"
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: political_guidance
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Focuses on AI capacity-building, digital divides, infrastructure, skills, and developing-country participation in AI governance.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Global Digital Compact (Pact for the Future)
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: political_guidance
treatyParticipationCards.summary: First UN comprehensive framework for digital cooperation and AI governance. Commits states to UN-level mechanisms for AI governance dialogue, risk mitigation, human oversight, and capacity-building. Negotiated by 193 UN member states.

treatyParticipationCards.title: UN General Assembly Resolution 79/325 — Independent International Scientific Panel on AI and Global Dialogue on AI Governance
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: political_guidance
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Formally establishes a 40-member independent scientific panel and a global dialogue process on AI governance. Most concrete institutional step the UN has taken toward standing AI governance machinery.

treatyParticipationCards.title: OECD Recommendation on AI / OECD AI Principles
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Most influential intergovernmental AI policy instrument, setting principles on inclusive growth, human rights, transparency, robustness, and accountability. Revised in May 2024 to reflect generative AI. Adhered to by 38 OECD members + EU + 8 non-member adherents.

treatyParticipationCards.title: G20 AI Principles
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: G20 AI-principles annex to the Ministerial Statement on Trade and Digital Economy. Effectively internationalized the OECD approach across major economies.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence declarations and ministerial statements
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Series of GPAI Council outcome documents giving ministerial-level direction to the Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence work programme. Included as AI policy coordination context, not as a treaty or binding legal instrument.

treatyParticipationCards.title: G7 Leaders' Statement on the Hiroshima AI Process
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Political umbrella for the G7 frontier-AI governance package. Explicitly addresses advanced AI systems including foundation models and generative AI, and endorses the follow-on Guiding Principles and Code of Conduct.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Hiroshima Process International Guiding Principles for Organizations Developing Advanced AI Systems
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: One of the clearest international texts aimed directly at frontier-model developers. Covers risk identification, red-teaming, incident reporting, cybersecurity, watermarking/authentication, public reporting, and post-deployment monitoring.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Hiroshima Process International Code of Conduct for Organizations Developing Advanced AI Systems
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Translates the Hiroshima principles into concrete organizational actions on governance, safety testing, incident handling, transparency, model-weight security, content provenance, and information-sharing.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Hiroshima AI Process Reporting Framework
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Operationalizes the Hiroshima Code by giving companies a structured way to disclose governance, risk-management, incident handling, and safety practices for advanced AI systems.

treatyParticipationCards.title: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689)
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: binding_regulation
treatyParticipationCards.summary: First binding, risk-based AI law including provisions on general-purpose AI models. In force from 1 August 2024 with a phased implementation timeline. Has extraterritorial commercial effect.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: binding_on_parties
treatyParticipationCards.summary: First international legally binding treaty on AI. Requires that the AI lifecycle be compatible with human rights, democracy and the rule of law; mandates risk and impact assessments, remedies, transparency, and a follow-up mechanism. Adopted 17 May 2024; opened for signature 5 September 2024. Treaty Office status checked 5 June 2026: not yet in force (needs five ratifications including at least three Council of Europe member states); EU ratification is recorded on 15 May 2026.

treatyParticipationCards.title: CEN-CENELEC AI Act harmonized-standardization work
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: standard
treatyParticipationCards.summary: European standardization workstream developing harmonized standards in support of the EU AI Act, including risk management, quality management, conformity assessment, transparency, human oversight, cybersecurity, and related operational topics.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Council of the EU conclusions on strengthening the EU's ambitions in AI
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: political_guidance
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Council conclusions calling for stronger EU leadership in trustworthy and human-centric AI, international standards work, talent, innovation, and global governance outreach. Soft law / political guidance.

treatyParticipationCards.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Artificial intelligence — Management system
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: standard
treatyParticipationCards.summary: World's first AI management-system standard. Establishes requirements for organizations to govern AI across the lifecycle. Widely used in audits, procurement, and enterprise assurance.

treatyParticipationCards.title: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — AI — Guidance on risk management
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: standard
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Voluntary international standard giving organizations a structured way to manage AI-specific risks across development, deployment, and use.

treatyParticipationCards.title: ISO/IEC 38507:2022 — Governance implications of the use of AI by organizations
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: standard
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Board-level governance standard explaining the governance implications of AI use by organizations.

treatyParticipationCards.title: ISO/IEC 22989:2022 — AI concepts and terminology
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: standard
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Foundational terminology standard for AI. Shapes interoperability of subsequent standards, procurement language, and policy drafting.

treatyParticipationCards.title: ISO/IEC 42005:2025 — AI system impact assessment
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: standard
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Standard focused on identifying and documenting AI system impacts on individuals, groups, and society.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Bletchley Declaration
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: First major international declaration centred on frontier-AI safety risks. Legitimised frontier-AI risk as an object of international coordination and triggered the State of the Science work and AI Safety Institute track. New Zealand later joined the participants.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Seoul Declaration for safe, innovative and inclusive AI
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Continues the Bletchley process and broadens it beyond safety to include innovation and inclusivity. Links frontier-model safety to democratic values, privacy, human rights, and bridging AI divides.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Seoul Statement of Intent toward International Cooperation on AI Safety Science
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Annex to the Seoul Declaration that formalises cooperation on AI safety science and acknowledges the role of state-supported AI Safety Institutes.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Seoul Ministerial Statement for advancing AI safety, innovation and inclusivity
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: More operational than the leaders' Seoul Declaration. Translates the summit vision into practical steps on governance, collaboration, safety, and sustainable development. Broader geographic participation.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Frontier AI Safety Commitments (AI Seoul Summit 2024)
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Voluntary company commitments announced in the AI Seoul Summit process. Participating organizations undertake to manage severe frontier-AI risks, publish safety frameworks, use risk thresholds, explain mitigations, and provide transparency where possible.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI for People and the Planet
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Adopted at the Paris AI Action Summit. Shifts focus from pure safety to public-interest AI — access, openness, sustainability, labour, and environment. The official signatory list includes 63 countries plus the African Union Commission and the EU; the United States and United Kingdom do not appear on that list.

treatyParticipationCards.title: International Network of AI Safety Institutes (now International Network for Advanced AI Measurement, Evaluation and Science)
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Intergovernmental network for AI safety science, evaluation methods, and joint research. Launched by 10 countries plus the EU; refocused and renamed on 9 December 2025.

treatyParticipationCards.title: ASEAN Guide on AI Governance and Ethics
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Regional, interoperability-oriented governance toolkit for trustworthy commercial AI in non-military settings. Risk-based governance, human oversight, explainability, incident management, and internal governance structures.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Expanded ASEAN Guide on AI Governance and Ethics — Generative AI
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Extends the 2024 ASEAN guide to generative AI. Covers synthetic content, model risk, misuse, transparency, copyright, bias, privacy, and operational governance.

treatyParticipationCards.title: African Union Continental AI Strategy
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: political_guidance
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Continental, development-focused AI strategy emphasising digital sovereignty, infrastructure, data, skills, innovation ecosystems, inclusive growth, and African representation in global AI governance.

treatyParticipationCards.title: APEC Digital and AI Ministerial Statement
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Links AI governance to digital trade, interoperability, standards, capability-building, and safe AI use across the Asia-Pacific.

treatyParticipationCards.title: APEC AI Initiative 2026–2030
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Five-year regional framework on AI innovation, governance, talent, interoperability, and diffusion. Connects AI governance to infrastructure and economic competitiveness.

treatyParticipationCards.title: EU–U.S. Joint Roadmap on Evaluation and Measurement Tools for Trustworthy AI and Risk Management
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Bilateral roadmap to align EU and U.S. work on AI evaluation and measurement tools, terminology, taxonomies, and risk management approaches.

treatyParticipationCards.title: UK–U.S. Memorandum of Understanding on AI safety
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: non_binding
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Bilateral MoU between the UK and U.S. AI Safety Institutes (AISI / CAISI) on cooperation, joint evaluations, methods, personnel exchanges, and information-sharing.

treatyParticipationCards.title: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Cross-sectoral companion profile to NIST AI RMF 1.0 for generative AI. Gives organizations voluntary guidance for identifying, measuring, managing, and governing generative-AI risks.

treatyParticipationCards.title: Frontier Model Forum
treatyParticipationCards.subtitle: voluntary
treatyParticipationCards.summary: Industry forum coordinating frontier AI safety, security, thresholds, evaluations, and information-sharing among leading frontier-model companies. Not an intergovernmental instrument — included here to make the corporate-governance layer visible.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: The EU AI Act creates risk-based duties, including high-risk AI risk management and GPAI risk-management obligations under phased application.

obligationCards.title: transparency_disclosure
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: The EU AI Act includes transparency duties for specified AI systems and additional information duties for general-purpose AI model providers.

obligationCards.title: conformity_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: High-risk AI systems must satisfy conformity and quality-management requirements before placement on the EU market.

obligationCards.title: prohibited_practices
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: The EU AI Act prohibits specified AI practices, including certain manipulative, social-scoring, and biometric uses.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / conditional
obligationCards.summary: The CoE AI Convention requires parties to maintain measures addressing AI lifecycle risks and impacts on human rights, democracy, and rule of law.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: Korea's AI Basic Act creates a national framework for high-impact AI and governance duties before its 2026 application date.

obligationCards.title: registration_filing
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: China's generative-AI measures require providers of public-facing generative AI services to satisfy filing, security, and content-governance expectations.

obligationCards.title: watermarking_content_labeling
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: China's AI rule stack includes generated-content governance and labelling expectations for public generative-AI services.

obligationCards.title: safety_framework_publication
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: California SB 53 requires covered frontier developers to publish safety frameworks.

obligationCards.title: incident_reporting
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: California SB 53 requires covered frontier developers to report specified safety incidents.

obligationCards.title: safety_framework_publication
obligationCards.subtitle: voluntary / direct
obligationCards.summary: The Seoul Frontier AI Safety Commitments ask participating companies to publish safety frameworks, thresholds, and severe-risk management processes.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: guidance / direct
obligationCards.summary: The NIST GenAI Profile gives voluntary guidance for identifying, measuring, managing, and governing generative-AI risks.

obligationCards.title: data_governance
obligationCards.subtitle: standard / direct
obligationCards.summary: ISO/IEC 42001 provides AI management-system requirements for organizational governance across the AI lifecycle.

obligationCards.title: audit_bias_audit
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: NYC Local Law 144 requires annual independent bias audits for covered automated employment decision tools.

obligationCards.title: watermarking_content_labeling
obligationCards.subtitle: binding / direct
obligationCards.summary: The TAKE IT DOWN Act creates notice-and-removal duties for covered platforms handling nonconsensual intimate imagery, including AI-generated digital forgeries.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: proposed / conditional
obligationCards.summary: Australia's proposed mandatory guardrails would turn selected high-risk AI safety expectations into mandatory duties.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: proposed / conditional
obligationCards.summary: Brazil's PL 2338/2023 would create a risk-based AI framework with duties for developers and operators.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: proposed / conditional
obligationCards.summary: Türkiye's draft AI law would create risk-assessment and compliance expectations for high-risk AI systems.

obligationCards.title: transparency_disclosure
obligationCards.subtitle: proposed / conditional
obligationCards.summary: Mexico's proposed federal AI law is tracked as a proposal for AI governance duties, including transparency-oriented obligations.

obligationCards.title: risk_assessment
obligationCards.subtitle: conditional / conditional
obligationCards.summary: OpenAI's EU-facing general-purpose AI activity creates a conditional EU AI Act exposure hook captured in the lab exposure dataset.

obligationCards.title: compute_infrastructure_reporting
obligationCards.subtitle: indirect / indirect
obligationCards.summary: U.S. advanced-chip export controls are tracked as an infrastructure constraint affecting Chinese frontier-AI compute capacity.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: EU AI Act entered into force
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: in_force
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 entered into force on 1 August 2024, with phased application after that date.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: EU AI Act phased application
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: Next deadline: 2026-08-02
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: The Act applies through phased deadlines; the next broad deadline tracked here is 2 August 2026.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: EU member-state competent-authority deadline
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: regulator_appointed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: The Commission describes 2 August 2025 as the deadline for Member States to designate and empower national competent authorities under the AI Act.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: GPAI Code of Practice published
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: guidance_issued
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: The General-Purpose AI Code of Practice is tracked as voluntary EU implementation guidance for providers of GPAI models under the AI Act.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: CoE AI Convention adopted; not yet in force in snapshot
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: adopted
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: The Convention was adopted and opened for signature, but the snapshot notes that entry-into-force conditions had not yet been met.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Korea AI Basic Act adopted; application begins in 2026
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: Next deadline: 2026-01-22
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Korea's AI Basic Act is tracked as adopted with application beginning in 2026.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: China generative-AI measures in force
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: in_force
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: China's Interim Measures for generative-AI services are tracked as in force for public-facing generative-AI services.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: California SB 53 signed
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: in_force
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: California SB 53 is tracked as a binding state-level frontier-AI transparency law after signing.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Brazil PL 2338/2023 under consideration
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Brazil's AI framework bill remains tracked as proposed until enactment is verified.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Türkiye draft AI law under consideration
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Türkiye's draft AI law remains tracked as proposed pending official enactment.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Mexico federal AI proposal tracked
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Mexico's federal AI proposal is retained as proposed and not counted as binding law.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Norway AI Act implementation consultation
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Norway is tracked as proposed/implementation planning for EEA alignment with the EU AI Act.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: UK pro-innovation AI regulation response issued
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: guidance_issued
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: The UK cross-regulator principles approach is tracked as guidance and institutional implementation context, not as an omnibus AI statute.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: UK Artificial Intelligence (Regulation) Bill in House of Lords
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Private member's bill tracked as proposed; it is not counted as enacted UK AI law.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Italy Law No. 132/2025 in force
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: in_force
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Italy's national artificial-intelligence law is tracked as in force and includes competent-authority designations for EU AI Act implementation.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Italy designates ACN and AGID roles
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: regulator_appointed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: The Italian law record tracks ACN and AGID as competent national authorities for AI-system monitoring and inspection.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Slovenia EU AI Act implementation law in force
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: in_force
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Slovenia's EU AI Act implementation law is tracked as in force from the official PISRS record.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Germany draft EU AI Act implementation bill
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: German implementation activity is tracked as draft/proposed pending promulgation.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: France EU AI Act implementation activity tracked
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: implementing_rules_pending
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: French activity is tracked as implementation context and supervisory work; no comprehensive national implementation act is counted here.

implementationDeadlineCards.title: Spain draft AI law approved as preliminary draft
implementationDeadlineCards.subtitle: proposed
implementationDeadlineCards.summary: Spain's EU AI Act implementation draft is tracked as proposed and not as an enacted national AI law.
